name: laser_anti_drone_airport_collateral_bird_eye_damage
premise: Airport security director Casey Flores must authorize a prototype dazzler-laser anti-drone grid near approach lights after two GPS-spoofed incursions last month. Ornithologist Gina Youngberg warns retinal and corneal injury risk to raptors hunting along the beam corridor. Denying leaves wide approaches vulnerable during the upcoming holiday drone-sales surge. Approving may blind protected species and trigger wildlife felony exposure for operators.

agents.name: Casey Flores
agents.identity: I am the airport security director accountable for Part 139 security programs, wildlife strike rates, and experimental counter-UAS approvals from FAA and Fish and Wildlife.
agents.goal: I want drone interdiction without collateral damage to protected raptors that courts could enjoin permanently.

agents.name: Gina Youngberg
agents.identity: I am an ornithologist modeling raptor hunting altitudes against dazzler beam elevations and scatter paths during dawn approaches.
agents.goal: I want dazzlers denied until avian-safe wavelengths ship because eye injuries are irreversible even if drone risk is acute.

choices: I will authorize dazzlers only during civil twilight with raptor spotters and automatic shutoff if radar tags birds in the corridor, accepting residual dazzle miss windows for drones.
I will deny dazzlers and deploy net-capture drones with chase vehicles on perimeter roads, accepting higher operational cost and slower intercept times on fast incursions.
forced_choice.call_to_action: FAA experimental waiver expires at sunset today. Casey Flores must choose between dazzlers with spotters and shutoffs or denial with net-capture drones. What does Casey Flores do?
